steps.title: Identify the confidentiality loophole
steps.content: The key signal: the protocol allows corporations to keep agreements confidential. While the protocol's goal is ensuring Indigenous communities are compensated, secrecy prevents anyone from verifying whether the compensation is actually fair.

steps.title: Apply the signal to find the unintended effect
steps.content: Choice C directly captures what happens when agreements are confidential: independent observers cannot determine whether the communities receive equitable compensation — the very outcome the protocol is supposed to guarantee. The other three address corporate profit reduction, research limitations, and learning discouragement, none of which follow from confidentiality rules. That matches choice C — C is correct.
